etymology.description: 廣(廣) (guǎng, "broad") refers to a vast area; 州(州) (zhōu, "prefecture") indicates a region or administrative division.
etymology.interpretation: 廣(廣) (guǎng, "broad") highlights the geographic expansiveness or prominent status of the region. 州(州) (zhōu, "prefecture") reflects its historical and administrative significance as an important regional center. Together, 廣(廣) (guǎng, "broad") + 州(州) (zhōu, "prefecture") denotes a large and significant administrative area, which is true for Guangzhou as a major city in southern China.

examples.chinese: 广州(廣州)是一个大城市(是一個大城市)。
examples.pinyin: Guǎngzhōu shì yī gè dà chéngshì.

examples.chinese: 他们计划去广州(他們計劃去廣州)旅游(旅遊)。
examples.english: They plan to travel to Guangzhou.

examples.chinese: 广州(廣州)拥有许多美丽的景点(擁有許多美麗的景點)。
examples.pinyin: Guǎngzhōu yǒngyǒu xǔduō měilì de jǐngdiǎn.
